message:
[review-fix](ft-conformance): bound case.op echo in run_*_case error paths

Cross-review of frankentorch-0ji (eccb82c) found that the bounded
parser-diagnostic helper was applied to four parse_ helpers
(parse_binary_op, parse_dtype, parse_device, parse_keyset) but the
SAME class of unbounded-string-echo bug still lives in 20 other
"unsupported X op '{}'" format sites in the same file. Those sites
take case.op straight from JSON conformance fixtures (the exact same
fuzz/adversarial input surface the 0ji bead motivated bounding) and
print the full string into the error message — a single oversized
token in any fixture produces multi-megabyte error/log output.

Sites bounded (case.op now wrapped in bounded_parse_token):
- run_scalar_binary_case (line 4671 + duplicate at 8620)
- run_tensor_binary_case (4767)
- run_tensor_unary_case  (4891)
- run_tensor_comparison_case (4981)
- run_tensor_factory_case (5098)
- run_tensor_init_case   (5257)
- run_tensor_random_case (5468)
- run_tensor_reduction_case (5742)
- run_tensor_loss_case   (5841)
- run_tensor_linalg_case (6018)
- run_tensor_normalize_case (6096)
- run_tensor_elementwise_cmp_case (6185)
- run_tensor_shape_case  (6323)
- run_tensor_inplace_case (6416)
- run_tensor_advanced_case (6566)
- run_tensor_sort_case   (6671)
- run_tensor_indexing_case (6906)
- run_tensor_scan_case   (6979)
- run_tensor_join_case   (7082)
(20 sites total.)

Existing `parser_rejection_diagnostics_bound_oversized_tokens` test
transitively covers all 20 new sites via the bounded_parse_token
helper-level guarantee.

ft-conformance: 93 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
